introduction:
A Death Certificate in Telangana is issued by the GHMC, Municipality, or Gram Panchayat under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act. Telangana uses the CDMA (Commissioner and Director of Municipal Administration) portal and Dharani for property-related death registrations.

Essential for insurance claims, property mutation, bank settlement, pension, and succession proceedings. Applications via MeeSeva centers or the CRS portal.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Register within 21 days (free)
  • After 21 days: late fee + Registrar permission
  • After 1 year: RDO / Magistrate order
  • First copy FREE
  • Track at cdma.telangana.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  • Helpline: 040-23262596
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Note: Carry originals; submit self-attested copies.
Proof of Death
  • MCCD from hospital
  • Hospital death summary
  • Cremation / Burial receipt
  • FIR (unnatural death)
ID of Deceased
  • Aadhaar
  • Voter ID
  • PAN
  • Passport
  • Ration Card
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar of informant
  • Relationship proof
  • Address proof
Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit
  • RDO / Magistrate order (1 year+)
  • Non-availability certificate
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via CDMA / MeeSeva / CRS

Telangana uses CDMA portal, MeeSeva, and CRS for online death registration.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Portal
    Go to cdma.telangana.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Create account, verify via OTP.
  3. Select Death Registration
    Navigate to Services → Death Registration.
  4. Fill Form
    Enter deceased details, cause/place of death, informant info.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID proof. Max 200 KB
  6. Submit
    Note Reference Number.
  7. Verification
    Local Registrar verifies within 7–15 days.
Tip: GHMC area residents can also use the GHMC citizen portal for faster processing.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Offline at MeeSeva / Municipal Office

Visit the nearest MeeSeva center, GHMC, Municipality, or Gram Panchayat.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Office / MeeSeva
    Hours: Mon–Sat, 9 AM – 5 PM
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Get Form No. 2, fill accurately.
  3. Submit Documents
    Attach photocopies, carry originals.
  4. Get Receipt
    Receive Acknowledgement.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect within 7–15 days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Visit CDMA Portal or MeeSeva.

Download Certificate

Download digitally signed certificate from the portal.

Legal Use

Insurance, property (via Dharani), bank, pension, and succession.

Grievance

Call 040-23262596 or visit GHMC / municipal office.
